solution:

```python
class MKAverage:
    def __init__(self, m: int, k: int):
        self.m = m
        self.k = k
        self.data = []

    def addElement(self, num: int) -> None:
        self.data.append(num)

    def calculateMKAverage(self) -> int:
        if len(self.data) < self.m:
            return -1
        return round(sum(self.data[-self.m:]) / len(self.data[-self.m:])

# Your MKAverage object will be instantiated and called as follows:
# obj = MKAverage(3, 2)
# obj.addElement(1)
# result = obj.calculateMKAverage()
```

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW

Explanation:

The provided solution implements the MKAverage class with an initialization method that takes m and k as parameters. The addElement method appends the given number to the data list. The calculateMKAverage method checks if the length of the data list is less than m. If so, it returns -1. Otherwise, it calculates the sum of the last m elements of the data list and divides it by the length of the last m elements. The solution has a time complexity of O(n), where n is the number of elements in the data list. The space complexity is O(n), as the data list has a size of n. The energy implications are low, as the solution only performs a few operations and does not consume significant energy.
